ClassNotFoundException.reasoning: In Python, the equivalent of Java's `ClassNotFoundException` is `ModuleNotFoundError`. This exception is raised when a module could not be located by its name or path. In this case, it appears that the code is trying to load a class using its string name and if it fails to do so, it raises a `ModuleNotFoundError` instead of a `ClassNotFoundException`.
